revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 神神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=藤枝市}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]岡部町三輪1290 |位置 = {{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]]（[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]） |創建 = [[皇極天皇]]3年（[[644年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=神神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}}} }} '''神神社'''（みわじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]岡部町三輪にある神社<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 神神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 藤枝市東部、岡部町三輪地区の中心部に鎮座している。
創建は[[皇極天皇]]3年（[[644年]]）とされ、東国に疫病が蔓延した際に、退散祈願として、[[大和国]]（奈良県）の[[大神神社]]を勧請して創建したとされ、[[式内社]]としても記録されている古社である<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html 神神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm 神神社] - 玄松子</ref>。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[郷社]]に列格、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主神]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html 神神社] - 公式サイト * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 神神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{DEFAULTSORT:みわしんしや ふしえたし}} [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:藤枝市の建築物]] [[Category:藤枝市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:三輪神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 神神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=藤枝市}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]岡部町三輪1290 |位置 = {{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]]（[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]） |創建 = [[皇極天皇]]3年（[[644年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=神神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}}} }} '''神神社'''（みわじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]岡部町三輪にある神社<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 神神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 藤枝市東部、岡部町三輪地区の中心部に鎮座している。
創建は[[皇極天皇]]3年（[[644年]]）とされ、東国に疫病が蔓延した際に、退散祈願として、[[大和国]]（奈良県）の[[大神神社]]を勧請して創建したとされ、[[式内社]]としても記録されている古社である<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html 神神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm 神神社] - 玄松子</ref>。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[郷社]]に列格、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主神]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html 神神社] - 公式サイト * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 神神社] - 静岡県神社庁

revision.comment: fixing parameters on [[Category:Infobox religious building with unknown affiliation]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |name = 神神社 |image = {{image募集中|cat=藤枝市}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]岡部町三輪1290 |位置 = {{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[大物主神]] |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]]（[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]） |創建 = [[皇極天皇]]3年（[[644年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=神神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}}} }} '''神神社'''（みわじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]岡部町三輪にある神社<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 神神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 藤枝市東部、岡部町三輪地区の中心部に鎮座している。
創建は[[皇極天皇]]3年（[[644年]]）とされ、東国に疫病が蔓延した際に、退散祈願として、[[大和国]]（奈良県）の[[大神神社]]を勧請して創建したとされ、[[式内社]]としても記録されている古社である<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html 神神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm 神神社] - 玄松子</ref>。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[郷社]]に列格、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定<ref name="engi"/>。
== deity == * [[大物主神]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html 神神社] - 公式サイト * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 神神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{citation needed:みわしんしや ふしえたし}} [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:藤枝市の建築物]] [[Category:藤枝市の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:神神社 (藤枝市)]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

{{Shrine | Name = Miwa Shrine (神神社) | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Fujieda City}} | Location = {{ill|Miwajinja, Okabe, Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|岡部町三輪}}, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|藤枝市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, 1290 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E|display=title}} | Deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} ({{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}) | Established = 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇}}'s reign (644) | Head Priest = | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = October 19 | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Miwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}}} }} }}
'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|藤枝市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== Deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html Miwa Shrine] - Official website * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|藤枝市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}

{{Shrine | Name = Miwa Shrine (神神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujieda City}} | location = {{ill|Miwajinja, Okabe, Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|岡部町三輪}}, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|藤枝市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, 1290 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} ({{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}) | Established = 3rd year of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇}}'s reign (644) | Head Priest = | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = October 19 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Miwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}}} }} }}
'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|藤枝市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html Miwa Shrine] - Official website * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}} [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Fujieda, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Fujieda, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha shrines in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:null category]][[sn:Miwa Shrine (Shizuoka)]][[Category:null category]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Miwa Shrine (Shizuoka)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 1]] [[ja:神神社 (藤枝市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Miwa Shrine (Shizuoka)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|藤枝市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|藤枝市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}

The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|ja|藤枝市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}} (1873), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}

{{神社 |名称 = 神神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=藤枝市}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]岡部町三輪1290 |位置 = {{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大物主神]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[郷社]]（[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]） |創建 = [[皇極天皇]]3年（[[644年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=神神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}}} }} '''神神社'''（みわじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]岡部町三輪にある神社<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 神神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 藤枝市東部、岡部町三輪地区の中心部に鎮座している。
創建は[[皇極天皇]]3年（[[644年]]）とされ、東国に疫病が蔓延した際に、退散祈願として、[[大和国]]（奈良県）の[[大神神社]]を勧請して創建したとされ、[[式内社]]としても記録されている古社である<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html 神神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm 神神社] - 玄松子</ref>。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[郷社]]に列格、明治40年（[[1907年]]）に[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]に指定<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主神]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html 神神社] - 公式サイト * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 神神社] - 静岡県神社庁

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

== deity ==

* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}

== References ==

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

== deity ==

* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}

== References ==

{{Reflist}}

== External Links ==

* [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html Miwa Shrine] - Official website
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

== deity ==

* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

== deity ==

* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

== deity ==

* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}

Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

== deity ==

* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}

== References ==

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

== deity ==

* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}

== References ==

{{Reflist}}

== External Links ==

* [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html Miwa Shrine] - Official website
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

== deity ==

* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.

According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>

== deity ==

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html Miwa Shrine] - Official website * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}}

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==

Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
== References ==

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html Miwa Shrine] - Official website * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}}

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [https://miwajinja.jp/index2.html Miwa Shrine] - Official website * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|53|56.3|N|138|17|47.6|E}}}} }} }}
'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda, Shizuoka|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Empress Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==

The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}

'''Miwa Shrine''' (神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|qid=Q734432|lt=Fujieda, Shizuoka|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, in the Okabe Town Miwa district.<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4407192 Miwa Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Office</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located in the eastern part of Fujieda City, at the center of the Okabe Town Miwa area.
According to tradition, Miwa Shrine was founded in the 3rd year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|ja|皇極天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōgyoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 斉明天皇|1=Empress Kōgyoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q235296}} (644). During an epidemic in the eastern provinces, the shrine was established to pray for the disease’s retreat. It is said to have been founded as a branch of {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|qid=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} (modern-day Nara Prefecture). The shrine is recorded as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, making it an ancient and historically significant shrine.<ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/suruga/bun/sr130101-01.html Miwa Shrine] - Engishiki Shrine Research</ref><ref name="genbu">[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/miwa_title.htm Miwa Shrine] - Genbu</ref>
In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1873), the shrine was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Later, in the 40th year of Meiji (1907), it was designated as a {{ill|Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen heihakuryō kyoshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, a shrine authorized to receive state offerings.<ref name="engi"/>
== deity ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}
